howItWorks.heading: three ways to
howItWorks.step1Desc: tell driftd how you're feeling today — and how long you have.
howItWorks.step1Detail: every route is unique. generated in seconds. never the same twice.
howItWorks.step2Desc: driftd becomes your AI audio companion — it narrates your walk with stories, history, and secrets.
howItWorks.step2Quote: like having a friend who grew up here whispering in your ear
howItWorks.step3Desc: no staring at your phone. no following arrows. driftd guides by voice and nudges you when something cool is nearby.
howItWorks.step3Quote: you didn't follow a route. you drifted.

featureTicker.p5: stories you won't find anywhere else

mapDemo.caption: AI-generated · every route is one of a kind · never repeats
mapDemo.captionMobile: AI-generated · every route is one of a kind

aiCompanion.heading: your city has stories.
aiCompanion.body: most travel apps give you lists. driftd gives you a voice — one that knows the neighborhood, remembers what you've seen, and gets more interesting the further you wander.
aiCompanion.badge: driftd · real-time AI · no scripted tours · no app switching
aiCompanion.m1Desc: start a route, put in your headphones, and drift. driftd narrates your walk with stories, history, and local secrets as you pass each spot — not a tour script, but a conversation.
aiCompanion.m1Detail: you pick a neighborhood and a vibe, driftd generates a walking route and narrates each stop as you arrive. stories, context, recommendations — all in real time, all felt like a local friend telling you about their city. no scripted audio, no stiff museum voice. just good conversation.
aiCompanion.m2Desc: see a building that catches your eye? ask driftd. it figures out what you're looking at and tells you the story behind it — the thing no signpost ever would.
aiCompanion.m2Detail: point your phone at anything — a building, a mural, a market stall, a weird statue — and ask driftd what it is. it uses your location and camera context to figure out exactly what you're looking at, then gives you the story behind it. the kind of detail that takes hours of googling, delivered in seconds.
aiCompanion.m3Desc: not following a route? just walking? driftd watches your location and nudges you when something interesting is nearby. say yes or keep walking — that's the whole point.
aiCompanion.m3Detail: no plan, no pressure. just turn it on and walk. driftd runs quietly in the background, tracking where you are, and taps you when something worth knowing about is within reach — a courtyard most people miss, a bar that's been there since 1962, a mural that appeared last week. you decide whether to detour or drift on.

manifesto.l5: the afternoon you couldn't have planned.

forCities.body: overcrowded landmarks, underfunded neighborhoods. driftd redistributes tourist foot traffic intelligently — sending curious people to the places that deserve them, not just the ones that already have signs.
forCities.statText: of tourists in major cities never leave the main tourist corridor. driftd changes that.
forCities.prop1Desc: driftd routes tourists to undervisited neighborhoods, reducing pressure on overcrowded hotspots. tourists discover more. locals deal with less.
forCities.prop2Desc: partner businesses in quiet neighborhoods see real, measurable increases in foot traffic. driftd routes discovery — not just tourists.
forCities.prop3Desc: real-time analytics on tourist movement, preferences, and spending patterns — so cities can make evidence-based decisions about tourism policy.

antiFaq.heading: go on, we've
antiFaq.a0: Google Maps wants you to find things. we want you to find things you didn't know you were looking for.
antiFaq.a1: safer than following a tour group into a restaurant with photos on the menu.
antiFaq.q2: isn't this just a travel blog?
antiFaq.a2: travel blogs are written by people who got paid to be there. driftd is built by people who got tired of those blogs.
antiFaq.q3: what if the places are closed?
antiFaq.a3: most of our best stops don't have opening hours.
antiFaq.a4: it's for anyone who thinks the version of a city in a guidebook is a lie.
antiFaq.q5: what if i don't speak the language?
antiFaq.a5: the best discoveries happen before you figure out what to say.

waitlist.citySubtitle: your vote shapes where we launch.

footer.brand: driftd is anti-tourist tourism — an AI companion that shows you the city nobody else sees.
footer.newsletterBody: city launches, new features, and things we found wandering.

about.s1p0: 73% of visitors in major cities never leave the main tourist corridor. local neighborhoods that want visitors get none. overcrowded landmarks get worse every year.
about.s1p1: cities are desperate for solutions — imposing entry fees, running "don't visit" campaigns, raising tourist taxes. we think there's a better way.
about.s2p0: driftd uses AI to redistribute tourist foot traffic from overcrowded landmarks to undervisited neighborhoods.
about.s2p1: tourists get a better experience. local businesses get customers. cities get relief. everyone wins.
about.s3Heading: built by someone who got lost on purpose.
about.s3p0: built by dawid — a yale student studying cognitive science and economics, with experience in investment banking and public policy.
about.s3p1: driftd combines a background in decision science with a belief that cities are best experienced without a plan.
about.s4Heading: infrastructure for the age of AI tourism.
about.s4p0: we're starting with three cities. but driftd isn't just a travel app — it's infrastructure for how cities manage tourism in the age of AI.
about.s4p1: we're building the demand-shaping layer between tourists and the places they visit. starting small. thinking big.
about.ctaHeading: curious to see the real city?
about.ctaBody: join the waitlist and be first to explore when we launch.
about.ctaMicro: launching summer 2026 in warsaw, berlin & prague
